content:
There is a much bigger past happening that we should never forget and it is already slipping out of the social conscious: "[The Holocaust] was the greatest crime of the 20th century, but Gen Z and millennial Americans remain hugely ignorant about [it]. One in 10 young Americans believes that the Holocaust never happened, while almost a quarter — 23% — believe it is a myth or that the number of Jews who were killed has been exaggerated" (vice.com). When a society forgets about its past transgressions that society is doomed to repeat the same mistakes.

In the early 20th century, Germany, Italy, and Japan caught a severe case of Nationalism and Fascism and then went on to set the entire world on fire.

In the early 21st Century (AKA Today), Nationalism and Fascism (disguised in a different set of stage clothes) is spreading once again...not only over seas, but on our own soil as well.

As George Carlin foretold, "When fascism comes to America, it will not be in brown and black shirts. It will not be with jack-boots. It will be Nike sneakers and Smiley shirts." And we are not doing a single thing about it. We are fat and comfortable Americans who never had to fight for anything other than a place in a decent college or for a coveted job at Google. In other countries the people riot when their rights are taken from them or when democracy fails them. Most of us do not. We do not go to bed at night worried about being collateral dead by a drone strike that was meant for someone else who may have been involved in something that might've happened.
context: From document: On the yearly anniversary of 9/11 Americans all over the country share… — chunk 2 (chunk 2/4)
